review_snippets.title: Asana Review--The Good and Bad - Crazy Egg
review_snippets.content:
Can’t assign tasks to multiple users: Asana doesn’t allow project managers to assign tasks to more than one user. In theory, this is designed to make it clear exactly who is responsible for a particular task. You can add a task collaborator or use subtasks to bring multiple users into the loop. Asana also lets you create copies of tasks for other users. But at the end of the day, tasks will only have one main assignee.

Email clutter: Many Asana users complain about the number of notification em

One idea: audit a single process (maybe how feedback travels between teams?) and look for the friction points. You might find that a few smart changes beat another shiny tool any day. [...] full of fresh ideas, building mini tools to manage PM tasks, and turning complex concepts into working magic.   Rabeea Hussain, our 𝐭𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, stepped into a completely new role with confidence - acing her testing through Postman and keen attention to detail.   I and Javeria Amir teamed up for 𝐔𝐈/𝐔𝐗 𝐝𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐦𝐚𝐫𝐤𝐞𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 - combining creativity and analytical thinking to make NafaVerse not just functional, but also visually meaningful and user-centered. And of course, heartfelt thanks to our course instructor Miss Maryam Feroze and Sir Syed Yawar Abbas for the mentorship, guidance, and fun-filled lessons that made learning truly memorable. We’d love for you all to check out our website  and share your thoughts - your feedback means a lot! Visit our Contact page to drop in your suggestions.

[Asana Review 2025: Is It Worth It for SaaS Startups? - SaasPedia]: ### How I Ranked These Softwares

| Factor | Weight |
 --- |
| Ease of Use | 20% |
| AI & Automation | 20% |
| Integrations | 20% |
| Pricing Fairness | 20% |
| Scalability & Support | 20% |

I combined firsthand testing with user feedback from G2 (2,000+ reviews) and community discussions on Reddit’s r/SaaS and r/Productivity.  
Asana ranked #1 overall for automation reliability and #2 for ease of use, just behind ClickUp.

## FAQs

Yes, Asana offers a free plan for up to 10 members with basic features. Startups can also apply for discounts through Asana’s Startup Program.

ClickUp offers more customization and cheaper pricing, but Asana wins in reliability, UX, and automation accuracy.

Yes. Asana AI automates task summaries, progress updates, and workload predictions. [...] Why I Chose It: Asana’s balance of structure and flexibility makes it ideal for teams scaling fast. It supports complex workflows (product launches, sprint cycles) but remains approachable for marketing and support.

Testimonials: According to G2’s 2025 grid, Asana scored 4.6/5 overall with the highest marks for “ease of collaboration.”

My Experience: During testing, I especially liked the AI-generated task summaries. When my team updated subtasks, Asana summarized changes automatically, saving me time during sync meetings.

### AI Capabilities:

### Alternatives:

#### ✅ Pros

#### ⚠️ Cons

Case Study: A 2024 SaaS firm, Acme.io, reported saving 12 hours per week per manager by automating weekly reporting with Asana AI workflows.

### How I Ranked These Softwares

[Asana Review: Is It Worth It in 2026? [In-Depth]]: ‘’One of my favourite pieces of Asana is the dashboard. It's customisable with different widgets so you can organise your workspace with a quick view of things like assigned tasks, pending statuses (colour-coded by severity), forms, projects, a private notepad to jot down notes, and others. You can flip between a weekly view and a monthly view. It has a functional app that mirrors the desktop version.’’ - G2 Review.

Common complaints:

 Limited to one assignee per task.
 Timeline view can be confusing with subtasks.
 Missing native time tracking.
 Higher-tier plans are needed for essential features. [...] Rating: 8/10.

Asana offers a comprehensive range of features alongside workflow automations – but the platform has limited collaboration capabilities due to their single assignee feature.

In Asana, you cannot assign a task to multiple team members.

I think that multi-assignees are necessary for collaboration. Otherwise, my team members would have to communicate via other platforms.

This adds an extra layer of complexity and can be counter-productive, as it forces workarounds.

That issue can also be seen in verified G2 reviews of Asana, which go over the fact that Asana doesn't support assigning work to a large group of individuals very well. [...] ‘’One downside to using Asana is that it doesn't support assigning work to a large group of individuals very well. Also, if you have many tasks to complete and need to move on to the next one, you must return to the previous one before starting the new one. This can take up considerable time, especially if you're under duress.’’ - G2 Review.

## Asana’s Interface: Is It Easy To Use?

Asana starts with a ‘’greeting’’ interface that provides you with an easy-to-understand overview of your outstanding tasks and projects that you’re involved in.

Inside the tasks, it’s easy to understand who’s responsible for what, the level of priority and the approvals needed – with a comment section for more details.
